dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires 'trend established over minimum 3 months' which cannot be directly encoded as a single condition; this should be enforced at backtest initialization via lookback_period_months=90. All other entry conditions (price > 100-SMA, SMA alignment, ADX > 25) are directly translatable. Exit conditions use standard trend reversal signals. Direction is LONG as the rule focuses on entering obvious uptrends.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires 'trend established over minimum 3 months' which cannot be directly encoded as a single condition; this should be enforced at backtest initialization via lookback_period_months=90. All other entry conditions (price > 100-SMA, SMA alignment, ADX > 25) are directly translatable. Exit conditions use standard trend reversal signals. Direction is LONG as the rule focuses on entering obvious uptrends.

source_rule_text: A trader should only enter a trade when the dominant long-term market trend is obvious and unambiguous, operationalized as a trend strong enough to be visually clear from distance.
